Flower Girls

The Flower Girl Style Guide: Crowns, Baskets, and What They Actually Need

By Michelle · 5 min read

Here is the truth nobody puts on the product page: a flower girl accessory has to survive a five-year-old.

I've made hundreds of crowns and pouches for small hands, and the difference between a piece that lasts through the reception and one that's in pieces by the processional comes down to construction choices you can learn to spot.

Crowns: comfort decides everything

A child will tolerate a crown for exactly as long as it doesn't bother her. That means: no exposed wire ends (feel the inside seam with your thumb), a slightly flexible band rather than a rigid hoop, and real weight limits — a crown over a few ounces will slide, tilt, and eventually get handed to a parent. Clay and fabric florals hold their shape all day; fresh flowers wilt by the ceremony's end and stain little fingers that can't stop touching them.

Sizing without the child present

You rarely get to fit the flower girl in person. Ask her parent to measure head circumference with a soft tape just above the ears — most girls between three and eight fall in a range a well-made adjustable ribbon closure covers easily. Ribbon ties beat elastic: they adjust on the day, they photograph beautifully trailing down the back, and they don't pinch.

The pouch-versus-basket question

Traditional baskets are lovely and awkward — wide, easy to swing, easy to spill. A small drawstring pouch solves most of it: petals stay put until she reaches in, the pearl handle gives small hands something definite to hold, and after the wedding it becomes a keepsake rather than storage-closet clutter. If your venue restricts loose petals (many do now), the pouch simply carries her "important things," which every flower girl understands perfectly.

What to skip

  • Fresh-flower crowns for children under six — beautiful for exactly forty minutes
  • Anything with glued-only embellishments; stitched survives, glue doesn't
  • Long sashes or trailing ribbons at trip height
  • Stiff headbands sized for adults

The rehearsal trick

Give her the crown and pouch at the rehearsal, not the wedding. One evening of wearing it makes the accessories familiar instead of fascinating — which means on the day itself, she walks instead of inspecting.

Every flower girl set in the atelier is built around these rules: stitched construction, adjustable ribbon closures, nothing sharp, nothing heavy. Made for photographs, sized for children, and honest about the difference.

Made to Order, In Your Colors

Every pouch, crown, and flower girl set is handcrafted after you order — in six signature shades or custom-matched to your palette.
